results.title: Emulation of Warlords: Battlecry for PC
results.creator_credit: Strategic Studies Group and Strategic Simulations, Inc.
results.brief_description: As part of the Warlords series, Warlords Battlecry retains the fantasy setting and tactical focus of its predecessors. However, if shifts from the wargame genre that the series is known for to a mix of real-time strategy and roleplaying. The players commands one of nine races – Human, Dwarf, Undead, Barbarians, Orcs, Minotaurs, High Elves, Wood Elves and Dark Elves. True to real-time strategy genre, buildings must be created, units trained, upgrades researched and resources gathered. Unique to this game is the addition of hero units, which can complete quests and gain experience, strength and skills, unlike the other units in their army.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Strategic Studies Group and Strategic Simulations, Inc.

results.title: Warlords III: Darklords Rising for PC
results.creator_credit: Strategic Studies Group and Red Orb Entertainment
results.material_description: CD-ROM videogame. CD-ROM, CD jewel case, advert, registration card, troubleshooting guide and manual in cardboard box
results.brief_description: A stand-alone expansion to Warlords III: Reign of Heroes, Warlords III: Darklords Rising adds new campaigns and scenarios, an enhanced random map generator, and more heroes, units, and spells. Gameplay has also been changed to allow for simultaneous movements, a first in the series.

results.title: NBA Jam Tournament Edition for Game Boy
results.creator_credit: Torus Games and Acclaim Entertainment Ltd
results.material_description: Cartridge videogame. Cartridge in plastic cartridge case
results.brief_description: An update from NBA Jam, the Tournament Edition continues to employ the fast and action-packed and unrealistic form of basketball from the original. Up to four players players can take part in the two versus two matches, with each controlling a single person for their team. The computer fills in for for missing players. The Tournament Edition increases each team’s roster to three, and allows for substitutions between quarters. Shoving players now results in injuries, which lower a player’s stats, which subbing recovers. New additions the Edition include randomized zones on the court that reward extra points for making a basket while standing within them, powerups that help with scoring or hurt the other team and Juice Mode, which increases the speed of the game.

results.title: Stargate for SNES
results.creator_credit: Probe Software, Tantalus Media and Acclaim Entertainment Ltd
results.brief_description: This action platform side-scroller set in the sci-fi universe of Stargate has the player rescuing the slaves of Abydos, defeating the evil villain Ra and getting his team back to Earth using the Stargate device. Most missions involve finding people or items in different locations, while fending off enemies with an upgradable machine gun and grenades. In addition, two missions has the player engaging in plane dogfights in one of Ra’s flying gliders. While the overall mission is to defeat Ra, seven bomb pieces and seven hieroglyphs must be collected beforehand or the player will lose the game even if they defeat Ra.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Probe Software, Tantalus Media and Acclaim Entertainment Ltd
results.description: This action platform side-scroller set in the sci-fi universe of Stargate has the player rescuing the slaves of Abydos, defeating the evil villain Ra and getting his team back to Earth using the Stargate device. results.title: The Lion King: Simba's Mighty Adventure for Game Boy Color
results.material_description: Cartridge videogame. Cartridge, plastic cartridge case, registration card, consumer information booklet and manual in cardboard box
results.brief_description:
Simba’s Mighty Adventure is based on Disney’s The Lion King as well as it’s direct-to-video sequel Simba’s Pride. The game consists of ten side-scrolling levels: The first six are set in The Lion King and the rest in Simba’s Pride, with some levels ending in boss fights. The game tracks along with the plot of both films and follows Simba as he grows up. Key events, such as the stampede from the first film, make up their own levels. Simba moves by running, jumping and climbing. Jumping can also be used to defeat the snakes, spiders, or monkeys that get in Simba’s way. He can also swipe at them with his claw or, as an adult, roar at them to make them flee.

Aiding Simba in his journey are collectibles that grant extra lives, refill or extend Simba’s health or fill a special amulet. Collecting all five parts of the amulet grants an extra life and opens up a mini-game that can be played main menu. The game also has a mulitplayer element. Using a Gameboy Colour link cable with another player opens up an extramode where each player has to hold onto a star for as long as possible by avoiding the opposing player’s attacks.

details.label: Materials

results.title: Play It Again II: Games Collection
results.brief_description:
The Play It Again II project aims to document, preserve, and create an accessible and playable history of Australasian videogames and videogame culture from the 1990s.

This collection comprises physical games and hardware donated to ACMI for the project and as well as game emulation files created by ACMI or harvested from external web repositories.

The project is being undertaken by ACMI in partnership with Swinburne University, RMIT, AARNet, UNESCO PERSIST and OpenSLX GmbHt.
This project is supported by the Australian Government through the Australian Research Council Linkage Program. Play It Again II is a continuation of Play It Again I, which focused on the preservation of Australasian videogames from the 1980’s.

results.title: South Park Rally for PlayStation
results.creator_credit: Tantalus Media and Acclaim Entertainment Ltd
results.material_description: CD-ROM videogame. CD-ROM and manual in CD jewel case with insert
results.brief_description: Based on the popular TV series, this kart-tyle racing game includes many of the characters from the show’s first two seasons. These include series mainstays like Stan, Kyle, Eric and Kenny to more unique characters like Mr. Garrison, Dr. Mephesto, and Terrance and Phillip, which have to be unlocked. While racing, a player can pick up different coloured boxes with a set of randomised items that can be used to boost the player or hinder other players. Other competitive game modes include variations of tag, collecting special items and delivering objects before other players do. Series creators Matt Stone and Trey Parker were not involved in the development of the game, but did provide voice work for the characters.

results.title: WipEout for Sega Saturn
results.material_description: CD-ROM videogame. CD-ROM and manual in Sega Saturn case
results.brief_description:
In the world of WipEout, anti-gravity racing is the world’s most popular sport. Tracks stretch out across the world and research is focused on finding even better and faster race craft. Racing for one of four corporations, the player competes to win the prestigious F3600 Anti-Gravity Racing League. Each corporation’s craft has its own strengths and weaknesses, which are further complicated by the choice of different pilots. Races aren’t simply affairs of speed and manoeuvring. The tracks are littered with weapons and powerups to hinder opponents or aid the player.

The game has three modes. Championship has the player attempting to place in the top three of each class, before proceeding to the next. If a player beats this mode in as the top of the Venom class, Rapier class, with tougher opponents, will be unlocked. Single Race pits the player against a full field of pilots in a single track. Time Trail is like single race, but without weapons.

results.title: Duke Nukem for Game Boy Color
results.brief_description: Duke Nukem’s world-saving exploits have attracted the attention of a new alien foe, the Zorgonites. They plan kidnap the smartest minds in the universe - including Duke’s - to power their hyper-intelligent war machine and conquer the universe. The game takes the player around the world and beyond to stop this from happening. A Game Boy Colour port of Duke Nukem II, much as been changed. For instance, the Zorgonites were originally called the Rigelatins. The levels, weapons, items, enemies, and bosses are almost completely different.
